title: Label 309 passphrase block
description: The passphrase-derivation block of a sealed-PoE envelope: a CLOSED CBOR map `{alg, salt, params}` describing how the content-encryption key is derived from a shared passphrase (instead of, and mutually exclusive with, per-recipient KEM slots). `alg` is the KDF identifier (open string; registry membership ENC_PASSPHRASE_ALG_UNSUPPORTED is a domain-pass check). `salt` is a 16..64-byte byte string (hex; below/above the bound emits ENC_PASSPHRASE_SALT_TOO_SHORT / ENC_PASSPHRASE_SALT_TOO_LONG). `params` is OPEN here (any map) — the validator narrows it on the registered `alg`. STRUCTURAL note: when `alg == argon2id`, `params` is the closed map `{m, t, p}` of CBOR unsigned integers; the memory/time/parallelism FLOORS (m >= 65536, t >= 3, p >= 1) and rejection of extra keys are domain-pass checks (ENC_PASSPHRASE_ARGON2_PARAMS_TOO_LOW / SCHEMA_UNKNOWN_FIELD), not asserted structurally here. The `argon2id-params` $def below documents that closed shape for reference.

salt.$comment: 16..64 bytes, modelled as a 32..128-character lowercase-hex string (each byte = 2 hex chars).

params.$comment: Open here; validator narrows on `alg`. For argon2id this is the closed {m,t,p} unsigned-integer map (see $defs/argon2id-params).

argon2id-params.title: Argon2id parameters (reference shape for alg == argon2id)
argon2id-params.description: Closed map of CBOR unsigned integers. Floors (m >= 65536, t >= 3, p >= 1) are NOT asserted here — they are domain-pass checks (ENC_PASSPHRASE_ARGON2_PARAMS_TOO_LOW). Shown for documentation; not $ref'd by the permissive `params` above so the structural superset stays algorithm-agile.
